title: English Judgment Structure Schema
description: Schema for segmenting English court judgments into standardised structural sections. Each section contains the verbatim text from the judgment. Sections that are not present in the document should be set to null.

case_identification.description: Case identifiers: neutral citation number, case number, court name, judgment date, court location. Extract the header block at the top of the judgment.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of key identifiers (citation, court, date)

court_composition.description: The bench: names and titles of judges hearing the case (e.g. 'Before: LORD JUSTICE GREEN, MR JUSTICE GARNHAM'). In UK judgments this typically appears after the court name and before the parties.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary listing judge names and roles

parties.description: Names and roles of the parties (appellant, respondent, defendant, Crown, co-defendants). In UK judgments: 'Between: REGINA - and - [Defendant Name]'. Include all named individuals.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of party names and their roles

legal_representation.description: Barristers, solicitors, and counsel appearing for each party. In UK judgments: 'Mr X QC (instructed by Y) for the Appellant'. Include all named representatives.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of who represented whom

procedural_history.description: The procedural background: prior proceedings, lower court decisions and dates, trial chronology, previous appeals. In UK appeal judgments this often appears in the opening paragraphs after the introduction (e.g. 'On [date] at [court], the appellant was convicted of...').

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

charges_or_indictment.description: The formal charges, counts on the indictment, offences charged. What the defendant was accused of. In UK judgments often stated as 'Count 1: conspiracy to supply drugs of Class A...' or woven into the procedural history.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of what was charged

operative_part.description: The actual ruling, order, or disposition. The binding decision of the court. In UK appeal judgments: 'The appeal is dismissed' or 'The conviction is quashed'. In sentencing: the sentences imposed. This is the most legally important section.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the court's decision

costs_and_fees.description: Orders on costs, legal aid fees, fee waivers. Relatively rare in UK criminal judgments but present in some cases. Set to null if no cost orders are mentioned.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

facts_of_the_case.description: The factual narrative: what happened according to the court's findings or the prosecution's case. The events, circumstances, and background. In UK judgments this is often the longest section, told chronologically in numbered paragraphs. Look for headings like 'The Facts', 'Background', 'The Case against the Appellant'.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: 2-3 sentence summary of the key facts

prosecution_case.description: The Crown/prosecution's theory, arguments, and evidence presentation as described in the judgment. If the prosecution case is not separately described from the facts, set to null. Look for 'The Crown's case was that...' or 'The prosecution contended...'.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the prosecution's position

defence_case.description: The defendant's version, arguments, and defence theory. Look for 'The appellant's case was that...' or 'The defence submitted...'. If merged with facts, set to null.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the defence's position

evidence_summary.description: Key evidence cited in the judgment: witness testimony, forensic evidence, documentary evidence, expert reports, CCTV, phone records. Only extract if there is a distinct evidence discussion section. If evidence is woven throughout the facts, set to null.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary of key evidence types

legal_framework.description: Statutes, Acts of Parliament, sentencing guidelines, and case law precedents cited by the court. Look for references to specific legislation (e.g. 'Sexual Offences Act 2003'), sentencing guidelines, or leading cases. If there is a dedicated 'The Law' section, extract it. Also extract jury directions on the law.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

summary.description: One-sentence summary listing key statutes and precedents

appeal_grounds.description: Specific grounds of appeal raised by each party. Extract as an array with one entry per ground. If this is not an appeal case, set to null.

ground_description.description: Brief description of what this ground argues

text.description: Verbatim text where this ground is stated

court_analysis.description: The court's legal reasoning and analysis. Extract as an array with one entry per issue or ground analysed. This is the core intellectual content of the judgment. In UK appeal judgments, the court typically addresses each ground of appeal in turn. Look for 'In our judgment...', 'We consider that...', 'The answer to this question is...'.

issue_label.description: The issue or ground being analysed (e.g. 'Ground 1: Inconsistent verdict', 'The safety of the conviction')

text.description: Verbatim text of the court's analysis of this issue

outcome.description: How the court resolved this issue (e.g. 'rejected', 'accepted', 'partially accepted')

sentencing_remarks.description: The court's reasoning on sentence: aggravating and mitigating factors, sentencing guidelines applied, starting point, reductions for guilty plea, totality principle. Set to null if sentencing is not discussed.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

conclusion.description: The court's closing summary before or including the final operative decision. Look for 'For these reasons...', 'In conclusion...', 'Accordingly...'. This wraps up the analysis and states the final result.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

dissenting_opinion.description: Any dissenting or minority judgment. Very rare in UK criminal appeals but possible. Set to null if there is no dissent.

text.description: Verbatim text of the dissenting opinion

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the dissenting view

ancillary_orders.description: Ancillary or supplementary orders: Sexual Offences Prevention Orders (SOPO), restraining orders, confiscation orders, compensation orders, disqualification, deportation recommendations. Set to null if none mentioned.

text.description: Verbatim text of this section from the judgment

section_count.description: The number of non-null sections identified in this judgment (out of 19 possible). This helps measure structural completeness.

structure_confidence.description: Your confidence in the structural segmentation. 'high' = clear section boundaries, headings present; 'medium' = sections identifiable but boundaries are fuzzy; 'low' = document is poorly structured or unusual format.
